name: The Sherwood State Bank
description_short: Community-focused bank with 4 branches in northwest Ohio offering personal and business checking, savings, loans, CDs, and mortgages with local service.
description_long:
Sherwood State Bank is a regional community bank serving four towns in northwest Ohio: Sherwood, Defiance, Archbold, and Fayette. The bank emphasizes local expertise and personal service as core to its mission, positioning itself around meaningful community connections rather than national scale. Founded on community-focused principles, the bank has built its identity around understanding the real lives and financial goals of local customers.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and business banking products. For consumers, this includes personal checking and savings accounts (marketed under their new "Sher-Advantage" product line), personal loans, certificates of deposit, mortgage loans, and debit cards with 24/7 ATM access. For businesses, they provide business checking and savings accounts, business loans, and agricultural lending. Their Sher-Advantage Checking requires a $500 minimum to open, offers unlimited transactions, and waives monthly fees with qualifying balance or direct deposit. The Sher-Advantage Savings account pairs with checking, requires $100 minimum, and earns monthly compounding interest.

What distinguishes Sherwood State Bank is its hyperlocal footprint and emphasis on personal lending guidance. All four branch locations maintain identical hours and offer 24/7 ATM access, drive-up service during business hours, and Saturday appointments. The bank explicitly markets straightforward loan terms and local lending team guidance rather than automated underwriting. Their mortgage products highlight competitive rates and automatic payments. This contrasts sharply with national and online-only banks by prioritizing face-to-face relationships and community accountability.

The primary limitation is geographic: Sherwood State Bank only operates in four specific Ohio towns, making it inaccessible to consumers outside this region. Digital capabilities appear minimal—the website is newly redesigned but offers no indication of online account opening, mobile banking, or digital lending tools. Interest rates and specific loan terms are not disclosed online, requiring customers to contact the bank directly. For consumers seeking modern digital banking experiences or comparing rates across institutions, this limitation is significant.
services: Personal Checking Accounts (Sher-Advantage Checking with $500 minimum)
Personal Savings Accounts (Sher-Advantage Savings with $100 minimum)
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) for short-term and long-term goals
Personal Loans with local lending team guidance
Mortgage Loans with competitive rates and automatic payments
Free Checking option with unlimited check writing
Credit Card Login (issuer status unclear from website)
pros: Four physical branch locations in Sherwood, Defiance, Archbold, and Fayette with 24/7 ATM access at each
Sher-Advantage Checking with $500 minimum, unlimited transactions, and no monthly fee with qualifying balance or direct deposit
Personal lending with local guidance and straightforward terms rather than automated systems
Saturday appointment-based banking available at all locations for working customers
Mortgage loans featuring competitive rates and automatic payment options
Business and agricultural lending programs tailored to community needs
Certificates of Deposit with options for both short-term and long-term financial goals
cons: Only serves four small towns in northwest Ohio—no access for consumers outside this geographic area
No interest rates, loan terms, or APR information disclosed on website; requires direct customer contact
No evidence of online account opening, mobile banking, or digital lending capabilities on website
Limited Saturday hours (by appointment only) and no Sunday banking at any location
No indication of online bill pay, mobile deposit, or other modern digital banking conveniences
best_for: Residents of Sherwood, Defiance, Archbold, or Fayette, Ohio seeking community-oriented banking relationships
Small business owners and farmers in northwest Ohio needing agricultural and commercial lending
Customers who prioritize in-person service and local lending decisions over digital convenience
Savers and borrowers comfortable calling or visiting branches to discuss rates and terms rather than comparing online
diagnosis: Sherwood State Bank is best for residents of its four-town service area in northwest Ohio who value personal relationships and local decision-making in banking. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation—this bank is entirely inaccessible to consumers outside Sherwood, Defiance, Archbold, and Fayette, and lacks modern digital banking features for remote account management.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
